akkhadhutto.meaning: a dice-gambler; one leading a wild gambling life
breakdown: Rogue, one of dissolute or wild life.

akkharapadabyañjanākāraniruttiniddesasam.meaning: with perfection of letter, word, consonant and vowel, of grammatical explanation, and of designation and description
akkharapadabyañjanākāraniruttiniddesasam.summary: A long compound listing the formal excellences of well-spoken teaching.
breakdown: Grammatical and etymological explanation of the words.

akkhātaṁ.gram: past participle of akkhāti, neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Root "to tell, declare" + past participle suffix -ta.

akkhātā.gram: past participle of akkhāti, plural or feminine form
breakdown: Root "to tell, declare" + past participle suffix -ta.

akkocchi.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of akkosati (√kus)

breakdown: Negative prefix "not" (with kk doubling by sandhi).
Despair, tribulation. (kodha + upāyāsa fuse to -dhū- by vowel sandhi.)

akkositabbo.meaning: to be accused, to be reviled; one deserving of abuse
akkositabbo.gram: future passive participle of akkosati, masculine nominative singular

akuppā.gram: adjective (potential participle of na kuppati), feminine nominative singular
breakdown: Liable to be shaken or disturbed (√kup "to be agitated" + -ya).

akā.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist/imperfect of karoti (√kar)

akāliko.meaning: giving results here and now; not delayed in time, immediate
akāliko.summary: One of the six qualities of the Dhamma recollected in the formula "svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo... akāliko...".
akāliko.goenka: Akālika is one of the qualities of the Dhamma: it gives its results here and now, without delay. In the tradition's understanding this is an experiential claim — the meditator who walks on the path begins to taste its fruits in this very life, not in some future existence only.

alasā,.gram: adjective, plural (paired in the glossary with its opposite analasā)
breakdown: Negative prefix in the paired opposite analasa, "not lazy".

alasā, analasā.meaning: idle, lazy; and its opposite: not lazy, diligent
breakdown: Negative prefix in analasa, "not lazy, diligent".

alañhi.meaning: truly, because; for indeed it is enough
alañhi.gram: sandhi fusion of particles: alaṁ (emphatic) + hi ("because, indeed")

alaṁpateyyā.meaning: surely should fit, would certainly suit; fit to be married
breakdown: Should fit, should suit (optative); also analysed as alaṁpateyya "fit for a husband (pati)".

alaṅkārānuppadānena.gram: neuter compound action noun, instrumental singular

allavattho.meaning: with wet clothes; wearing a wet garment

alliyati.meaning: to cling to, to adhere to
breakdown: Prefix "to, towards" (with ll doubling by sandhi).

amahaggata.meaning: not expanded, minor (of the mind: not grown great)

amanussa.meaning: a non-human being (spirit, deva or demon)
amanussa.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs together a list ("disease + non-human + famine") from a compound context (roga + amanussa + dubbhikkha); the sense of amanussa itself is "non-human being".

amaritukāmo.meaning: not wishing to die; desiring not to die

amarāvikkhepika.meaning: an "eel-wriggler"; one who practises endless equivocation
amarāvikkhepika.summary: From amarā, a fish difficult to catch, + vikkhepa "confusion, evasion": a debater whose answers cannot be pinned down.
breakdown: Name of a fish difficult to catch (an eel).

amataṁ.meaning: the deathless state; the deathless (an epithet of nibbāna)
amataṁ.gram: neuter noun (past participle of na marati), nominative/accusative singular
amataṁ.goenka: Amata, the deathless, is used in the suttas as an epithet of nibbāna — the state beyond arising and passing away, beyond birth and death. The meditator walking the path of sīla, samādhi and paññā is said to be moving towards this deathless state.

amatteyyatā.meaning: disrespect towards one's mother; absence of filial duty to the mother
breakdown: Dutiful, respectful towards one's mother (from mātā).

ambalaṭṭhikarāhulovādasuttaṁ.meaning: the Ambalaṭṭhikarāhulovāda Sutta, "the Advice to Rāhula at the Mango Grove (Ambalaṭṭhikā)" (sutta title)
breakdown: The Mango Grove, a place name.

amissībhāvo.meaning: the state of not being mixed; non-association, separateness
amissībhāvo.gram: masculine abstract noun, compound, nominative singular
breakdown: Mixed, associated (from missa, in the -ī-bhāva construction).

amittādummanā.meaning: without enmity and not sad; free of hostility and dejection

amutra.meaning: there, at another place; in that other existence

amutra/amūsa.gram: adverbial forms of the demonstrative pronoun amu

anabhirata.meaning: not indulging in, not finding delight in
breakdown: Delighting in, devoted to (past participle of abhiramati).

anabhisambuddhassa.meaning: of one not fully enlightened; before full enlightenment
anabhisambuddhassa.gram: past participle of na abhisambujjhati, genitive/dative singular

anabhāvaṁ.meaning: complete cessation of becoming; utter annihilation (of defilements)

anadhigatassa.meaning: of what has not been obtained or attained
anadhigatassa.gram: past participle of na adhigacchati, genitive/dative singular

anajjhāpatti.meaning: not incurring guilt; non-commission of offence
breakdown: Incurring guilt, committing an offence (adhi + āpatti).

anakkhātaṁ.gram: neuter past participle of na akkhāti, nominative/accusative singular
breakdown: Declared, announced (past participle of akkhāti, ā + √khā).

anakkosakaparibhāsako.meaning: one who does not revile or abuse
anakkosakaparibhāsako.gram: masculine agent noun, compound, nominative singular
breakdown: One who reviles, an abuser (from akkosati).
One who abuses, a slanderer (from paribhāsati).
